fields.description:
## Why

Epic 716f drained the module-size allowlist to zero — every over-cap module is split and no `src/rebar/**/*.py` exceeds 800 LOC. Retire the grandfathering ESCAPE HATCH so 800 becomes an absolute cap, and LOCK the limit so it cannot be changed through the normal contribution process (only an admin force-submitting past the gate can land a limit change). Per the operator decision this is a CI-gate-in-code mechanism (no Gerrit ACL).

Phase 2a is the enforcement SWAP. It cannot also delete the allowlist/ceilings files: the CI gate runs from MAIN's workflow against each change's tree, so main's CURRENT (allowlist-based) gate would break reading the deleted ceilings file on THIS change's own CI. So 2a rewrites the gate + tests and adds the limit single-source but KEEPS the now-empty allowlist/ceilings files; Phase 2b deletes them once the new gate is live.

## What

1. Add `.github/module-size-limit.txt` = `800` — the SINGLE SOURCE of the limit, read by the CI gate and the contract test.
2. Rewrite the `Module-size gate` step in BOTH `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` and `.github/workflows/test.yml` (same step NAME):
   a. Read `LIMIT` from the limit file (fail if missing or not a positive int).
   b. ABSOLUTE cap: fail if any `src/rebar/**/*.py` (excluding `__pycache__`) exceeds $LIMIT. No allowlist, no exemptions.
   c. LIMIT LOCK: fetch main's tip by EXPLICIT URL from the canonical GitHub repo — `git fetch --depth=1 "https://github.com/[github.repository]" '+refs/heads/main:refs/remotes/gh-main'` — the SAME explicit-URL pattern the tickets-branch fetch already uses in this very workflow, so it does NOT depend on an `origin` remote (checkout-gerrit-change-action does not guarantee one). Then `git cat-file -e refs/remotes/gh-main:<limitfile>` distinguishes: main HAS the file -> read BASE and FAIL with a 'must override' ::error:: if BASE != LIMIT (the lock); main LACKS it -> bootstrap (the file is introduced on THIS change) -> allow. If the fetch itself FAILS, emit a `::warning::` (NOT silent) and fall through to the absolute cap (always-on floor). So the lock fail-opens only on a total fetch failure and never mistakes a real base for bootstrap.
   d. MISSING-FILE GUARD: fail if the limit file is absent.
   e. Reads NEITHER the allowlist NOR the ceilings file in EITHER workflow.
3. Replace `tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py`: drop the allowlist/ceilings sync assertions; read the cap from the limit file; assert no `src/rebar` file exceeds it and the file parses to a positive int.
4. Update `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` `_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES` to `.github/module-size-limit.txt`.
5. KEEP the now-empty allowlist + ceilings files (Phase 2b deletes them) so main's current gate stays green on THIS change's CI.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.github/module-size-limit.txt` exists and contains `800` (verify: `test "$(cat .github/module-size-limit.txt)" = 800`)
- [ ] In BOTH workflows the gate reads the limit file and reads NEITHER the allowlist NOR the ceilings file (verify per file: `for f in .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml .github/workflows/test.yml; do grep -q 'module-size-limit.txt' "$f" || exit 1; ! grep -q 'module-size-allowlist' "$f" || exit 1; ! grep -q 'module-size-ceilings' "$f" || exit 1; done && echo OK`)
- [ ] In BOTH workflows the lock fetches main by explicit GitHub URL (not the `origin` remote), reads it via `git cat-file -e refs/remotes/gh-main`, and WARNS (not silently skips) on fetch failure (verify per file: `for f in ...both...; do grep -q 'github.repository' "$f" && grep -q 'cat-file -e refs/remotes/gh-main' "$f" && grep -q 'could not fetch main' "$f" || exit 1; done && echo OK`) — the explicit-URL pattern matches the existing tickets-branch fetch in the same file, so it is proven to work in this CI.
- [ ] The limit-lock override message is present in BOTH workflows (verify: `for f in ...both...; do grep -q 'must override' "$f" || exit 1; done && echo OK`)
- [ ] The gate FAILS when the limit file is MISSING (verify: run the gate step with no limit file -> exit 1 with 'must not be removed')
- [ ] A simulated limit change fails the lock; unchanged passes (verify: BASE=800 LIMIT=900 -> exit 1 with the override message; BASE=800 LIMIT=800 -> exit 0)
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py` passes (verify: `pytest tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py -q`)
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` passes (verify: `pytest tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py -q`)

## Notes
Phase 2b (follow-up) deletes the orphaned allowlist/ceilings files, prunes the docs/architecture.md offenders table, and updates AGENTS.md — separate because the gate must stop reading those files (this change) BEFORE they can be deleted. E5: the gate is shell (oracle = running it against crafted trees, per the ACs); the contract + parity tests are the Python oracle. The explicit-URL fetch is not an unverified assumption — it is the identical pattern the tickets-branch fetch (lines ~100/489 of gerrit-verify.yaml) already relies on, so its availability in this CI is established.
